subject: mathematics
context: Solve the following problems showing all working clearly.

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: Solve the quadratic equation $x^2 - 5x + 6 = 0$ by factorisation.

parts.label: b
parts.prompt: Hence find the values of $y$ when $y = 2x + 1$.

parts.label: c
parts.prompt: Verify your answer to (a) using the quadratic formula $x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$.

subject: mathematics
context: A vertical tower stands on horizontal ground. From a point P on the ground 24 m from the base of the tower, the angle of elevation to the top is 35°.

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: Draw a clear sketch showing the tower, point P, and the angle of elevation.
parts.answer: Right triangle with horizontal side 24 m, angle 35° at P, vertical side h (height).

parts.label: b
parts.prompt: Calculate the height of the tower correct to two decimal places.

parts.label: c
parts.prompt: From a second point Q, in line with P and the base, the angle of elevation is 50°. Find the distance PQ.

subject: mathematics
context: The marks obtained by 30 students in a mathematics test are summarised below. Marks: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50. Frequencies: 4, 7, 10, 6, 3.

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: Calculate the mean mark.

parts.label: b
parts.prompt: State the modal mark.
parts.answer: Mode is 30 (highest frequency = 10).

parts.label: c
parts.prompt: Find the median mark.
parts.answer: Median is the average of the 15th and 16th values. Cumulative: 4, 11, 21, … Both lie in the 30 class. Median = 30.

subject: science
context: A 12 V battery is connected to two resistors, $R_1 = 4 \Omega$ and $R_2 = 6 \Omega$, in series.

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: Calculate the total resistance of the circuit.

parts.label: b
parts.prompt: Calculate the current flowing through the circuit.

parts.label: c
parts.prompt: Calculate the potential difference across $R_2$.

parts.label: d
parts.prompt: State one advantage of connecting household appliances in parallel rather than in series.
parts.answer: Each appliance receives the full mains voltage; failure of one does not disconnect the others.

subject: science
context: When dilute hydrochloric acid is added to magnesium ribbon, hydrogen gas is liberated.

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: Write the balanced chemical equation for the reaction.
parts.answer: $\text{Mg} + 2\text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{MgCl}_2 + \text{H}_2$.

parts.label: b
parts.prompt: Identify the type of reaction.
parts.answer: Displacement (single replacement / redox) reaction.

parts.label: c
parts.prompt: Describe a simple test for hydrogen gas.
parts.answer: Bring a burning splint to the mouth of the test tube; hydrogen burns with a 'pop' sound.

parts.label: d
parts.prompt: Calculate the volume of hydrogen produced at STP when 2.4 g of magnesium reacts completely. (Mg = 24, molar volume = 22.4 dm³)
parts.answer: Moles Mg = 2.4/24 = 0.1 mol. Moles H$_2$ = 0.1. Volume = 0.1 × 22.4 = 2.24 dm³.

subject: science
context: The diagram of the human heart shows four chambers and major blood vessels.

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: Name the chamber that pumps oxygenated blood to the body.
parts.answer: Left ventricle.

parts.label: b
parts.prompt: State the function of the bicuspid (mitral) valve.
parts.answer: Prevents backflow of blood from the left ventricle into the left atrium.

parts.label: c
parts.prompt: Explain why the wall of the left ventricle is thicker than that of the right ventricle.
parts.answer: It must generate higher pressure to pump blood through the systemic circulation around the entire body, while the right ventricle only pumps to the nearby lungs.

parts.label: d
parts.prompt: Define 'double circulation'.
parts.answer: Blood passes through the heart twice in one complete circuit: once through the pulmonary circuit (heart-lungs-heart) and once through the systemic circuit (heart-body-heart).

subject: business-accounting
context: The following balances were extracted from the books of Lakshan Traders on 31.12.2025: Sales 480,000; Purchases 290,000; Opening stock 35,000; Closing stock 42,000; Salaries 48,000; Rent 24,000; Other expenses 18,000.

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: Calculate the cost of goods sold.
parts.answer: COGS = Opening stock + Purchases − Closing stock = 35,000 + 290,000 − 42,000 = 283,000.

parts.label: b
parts.prompt: Prepare the Trading and Profit & Loss Account in vertical format and calculate the Net Profit.

parts.label: c
parts.prompt: Calculate the Gross Profit ratio and comment.
parts.answer: GP ratio = 197,000/480,000 × 100 = 41.04%. Healthy margin; the firm controls cost of purchases well.

subject: business-accounting
context: On 30 June 2025 the cash book of Nimal showed a debit balance of Rs. 18,400. The bank statement showed a credit balance of Rs. 21,200. The following items were identified: (i) Unpresented cheques Rs. 4,800; (ii) A direct deposit by a customer of Rs. 2,500 not recorded in the cash book; (iii) Bank charges of Rs. 300 not yet entered.

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: Update the cash book and find the adjusted cash book balance.

parts.label: b
parts.prompt: Prepare a bank reconciliation statement starting from the adjusted cash book balance.
parts.answer: Adjusted cash book 20,600 + Unpresented cheques 4,800 − (Outstanding deposits, none) = Bank statement balance 25,400. (If 21,200 is shown, additional unrecorded items would be reconciled.)

parts.label: c
parts.prompt: Explain one reason why differences arise between the cash book and the bank statement.
parts.answer: Timing differences such as cheques issued by the firm but not yet presented for payment by the payee.

subject: business-accounting
context: A small bakery in Kandy plans to launch a new range of healthy bread products.

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: State two elements of the marketing mix.
parts.answer: Any two of: Product, Price, Place, Promotion.

parts.label: b
parts.prompt: Suggest two suitable promotional methods for the bakery and justify each.
parts.answer: Social media adverts (low cost, targets health-conscious youth) and in-store sampling (builds trust, encourages trial).

parts.label: c
parts.prompt: Explain why market segmentation is important before launching the new product.
parts.answer: It allows the bakery to focus on customers most likely to value healthy bread, design appropriate pricing and channels, and avoid wasting promotional budget on uninterested groups.
